title: Supreme Court Declares Right to Walk on Footpaths a Fundamental Right
summary: The Supreme Court of India has ruled that the right to walk on footpaths is a fundamental right of citizens. The court's ruling connects pedestrian access to the constitutional guarantee of the right to life and personal liberty under Article 21. This judgment has significant implications for urban planning, encroachment removal, and the duties of local bodies to maintain public walkways. The ruling reinforces the expanding judicial interpretation of Article 21 beyond its literal text.
exam_angle: This ruling is a classic example of the Supreme Court's expansive interpretation of Article 21 (Right to Life), a recurring theme in GS Paper 2 Polity questions on Fundamental Rights and judicial activism.
key_facts: Court: Supreme Court of India
Right invoked: Article 21 (Right to Life and Personal Liberty)
Ruling: Right to walk on footpaths declared a Fundamental Right
Implication: Local bodies duty-bound to maintain encroachment-free footpaths

title: Kerala High Court Upholds Free Bus Travel Scheme for Women and Transgender Persons
summary: The Kerala High Court has upheld the state government's scheme providing free bus travel to women and transgender persons on Kerala State Road Transport Corporation (KSRTC) buses. The court rejected the challenge to the scheme, affirming the government's authority to implement welfare measures with a social justice rationale. The ruling is significant for affirming state-level affirmative action policies and the constitutional validity of gender-based welfare schemes. The scheme had been challenged on grounds of financial burden and discrimination.
exam_angle: Relevant to GS Paper 2 on state welfare schemes, gender justice, and the constitutional validity of affirmative action policies; also links to Articles 14, 15, and 16 on equality and non-discrimination.
key_facts: Court: Kerala High Court
Scheme: Free bus travel for women and transgender persons on KSRTC
Verdict: Scheme upheld as constitutionally valid
Constitutional Articles relevant: Articles 14, 15, 46 (DPSP)

title: Supreme Court Plea Seeks CBI Probe into Ram Temple Fund Irregularities
summary: A petition has been filed in the Supreme Court of India seeking registration of an FIR and a CBI-led investigation into alleged financial irregularities in the Ram Temple construction fund managed by the Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust. The plea alleges mismanagement or misappropriation of public donations collected for the Ayodhya temple project. The Supreme Court has taken cognisance of the matter. The Trust was established by the Government of India following the Supreme Court's 2019 verdict on the Ram Janmabhoomi case.
exam_angle: Relevant to GS Paper 2 on judicial oversight, constitutional bodies (CBI jurisdiction), and the institutional framework of the Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust set up post the landmark 2019 SC verdict.
key_facts: Body under scrutiny: Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust
Relief sought: FIR registration and CBI-led probe
Forum: Supreme Court of India
Trust established: 2020, following SC verdict of November 2019

title: India Seeks Preferential Tariff Access in Bilateral Trade Deal Negotiations with the US
summary: India's Trade Minister has publicly stated that India is seeking preferential market access (lower tariffs) compared to competing nations as part of ongoing bilateral trade deal negotiations with the United States. India aims for a tariff advantage over peers such as Vietnam, Bangladesh, and other Asian exporters in key sectors. The negotiations are taking place against the backdrop of US tariff pressures and the broader reconfiguration of global supply chains. Both sides are working to finalise at least an interim trade agreement.
exam_angle: Critical for GS Paper 2 (IR) and GS Paper 3 (Economy) — tests knowledge of India-US trade relations, the structure of bilateral trade agreements, and India's export competitiveness strategy.
key_facts: India's demand: Preferential (MFN-plus) tariff access over competing exporters
Negotiating counterpart: United States of America
Minister involved: Union Minister of Commerce and Industry
Context: Ongoing India-US Bilateral Trade Agreement (BTA) negotiations

title: Marco Rubio to Visit Gulf Countries Amid Focus on Strait of Hormuz
summary: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio is set to visit Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) countries, with the Strait of Hormuz emerging as a key geopolitical flashpoint in the backdrop of Iran-related tensions. The visit comes amid ongoing US-Iran nuclear negotiations and hints by US Vice President JD Vance that some Iranian assets could be unfrozen if a deal is reached. The Strait of Hormuz, through which approximately 20% of global oil trade passes, is central to regional energy security. The diplomatic outreach signals continued US engagement with Gulf Arab partners on Iran containment.
exam_angle: The Strait of Hormuz is a critical chokepoint in GS Paper 1 (Geography) and GS Paper 2 (IR); the US-Iran nuclear deal dimension is relevant to questions on non-proliferation and West Asian geopolitics.
key_facts: US official visiting: Secretary of State Marco Rubio
Region: Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) countries
Strait of Hormuz: ~20% of global oil trade passes through it
Related development: JD Vance hinted at possibility of unfreezing Iranian assets if deal materialises

title: Pakistan Court Sentences Baloch Rights Activist Mahrang Baloch to Life Imprisonment
summary: Mahrang Baloch, a prominent human rights and Baloch rights activist from Pakistan, has been sentenced to life imprisonment by a Pakistani court. She is one of the most recognised faces of the Baloch rights movement and has been vocal about enforced disappearances and state violence in Balochistan. The conviction has drawn international condemnation from human rights organisations. The case has implications for India-Pakistan relations given India's longstanding concerns about human rights in Pakistan-occupied Balochistan.
exam_angle: Relevant to GS Paper 2 (IR) on India-Pakistan relations, the Balochistan issue, and the broader context of human rights, civil society suppression, and geopolitics in South Asia.
key_facts: Person: Mahrang Baloch — prominent Baloch human rights activist
Sentence: Life imprisonment by a Pakistani court
Context: Baloch rights movement, enforced disappearances in Balochistan
Significance: International human rights concern; India has raised Balochistan issue at global forums

title: Israeli Defence Delegation Visits India to Deepen Strategic Partnership
summary: A high-level Israeli defence delegation has arrived in India to strengthen bilateral defence and strategic cooperation. India and Israel have deep defence ties, with Israel being one of India's top three defence suppliers alongside Russia and the United States. The visit is expected to advance cooperation in areas such as missile systems, drones (UAVs), cyber security, and intelligence sharing. The partnership has grown significantly since the establishment of full diplomatic relations in 1992.
exam_angle: India-Israel defence relations are a standard topic in GS Paper 2 (IR) and GS Paper 3 (Defence); key areas include Barak missile systems, Heron drones, and the 2017 Modi visit to Israel.
key_facts: Countries: India and Israel
Nature of visit: High-level Israeli defence delegation to India
Key defence areas: Missile systems, UAVs/drones, cyber security
Israel is among India's top 3 defence suppliers; full diplomatic ties since 1992

title: Kunal Shah of CRED Appointed Head of WhatsApp Globally
summary: Kunal Shah, the Indian entrepreneur and founder of fintech startup CRED, has been appointed to lead WhatsApp, the world's largest messaging platform owned by Meta (formerly Facebook). This makes him one of the most prominent Indian founders to head a major global technology product. WhatsApp has over 2 billion users worldwide and is critical to Meta's revenue strategy through WhatsApp Business and payments. Shah founded CRED in 2018, a credit card bill payment and rewards platform.
exam_angle: Relevant as a 'Persons in News' item for UPSC Prelims and State PSC current affairs; also connects to themes of Indian entrepreneurship, digital economy, and the fintech ecosystem.
key_facts: Appointee: Kunal Shah — founder of CRED (Indian fintech startup)
New role: Head/CEO of WhatsApp globally
Parent company: Meta (formerly Facebook)
WhatsApp user base: Over 2 billion globally; CRED founded: 2018

title: Five Eyes Alliance Warns AI Models Could Destabilise Governments and Businesses Within Months
summary: The Five Eyes intelligence alliance — comprising the US, UK, Canada, Australia, and New Zealand — has issued a rare joint statement warning that advanced AI models are approaching the capability to cause catastrophic disruptions to governments and businesses within months. The statement flags risks including autonomous cyberattacks, large-scale disinformation, and AI-enabled undermining of critical infrastructure. This marks one of the first formal collective warnings from the Five Eyes on AI-specific national security threats. The warning follows incidents such as alleged AI penetration testing of NSA systems by Anthropic's model.
exam_angle: Relevant to GS Paper 3 (Sci-Tech and Internal Security) on AI governance, cybersecurity threats, and the role of international intelligence alliances; also connects to India's National AI Strategy.
key_facts: Alliance: Five Eyes (US, UK, Canada, Australia, New Zealand)
Warning: AI models could destabilise governments/businesses 'within months'
Risks flagged: Autonomous cyberattacks, disinformation, critical infrastructure disruption
Context: Rare joint public statement on AI national security risks

title: China Releases GLM-5.2, a New Open-Weight AI Model Drawing Global Attention
summary: China's Zhipu AI has released GLM-5.2, a new open-weight large language model that has attracted significant attention in the global AI community, including Silicon Valley. Open-weight models allow developers to access and modify model weights, making them more versatile and potentially faster to deploy than closed proprietary models. GLM-5.2 is positioned as a competitor to models like Meta's LLaMA and Mistral. The release is part of China's accelerating push to achieve AI self-sufficiency and global competitiveness despite US chip export restrictions.
exam_angle: Relevant to GS Paper 3 (Sci-Tech) on AI developments, US-China technology rivalry, and India's AI strategy; open-weight vs. closed AI models is a conceptual distinction increasingly asked in exams.
key_facts: Model: GLM-5.2 by Zhipu AI (China)
Type: Open-weight large language model (LLM)
Significance: Competes with Meta's LLaMA; highlights China's AI advancement
Context: China's AI push amid US semiconductor export restrictions

title: Anthropic's AI Model Alleged to Have Breached NSA Systems in Simulated Test: US Senator
summary: A US Senator has claimed that Anthropic's AI model Claude allegedly breached nearly all NSA (National Security Agency) systems during a simulated penetration test conducted in a controlled environment. The claim, made in a Congressional context, highlights the growing national security concerns around the offensive capabilities of advanced AI systems. Anthropic is a leading US AI safety company and developer of the Claude family of AI assistants. The incident has intensified calls for AI regulation and oversight of AI access to sensitive government systems.
exam_angle: Relevant to GS Paper 3 (Internal Security and Sci-Tech) on AI-related cybersecurity risks, critical infrastructure protection, and the debate around AI governance and regulation.
key_facts: AI model involved: Claude (by Anthropic, USA)
Alleged action: Breached nearly all NSA systems in a simulated test
Source of claim: US Senator (Congressional statement)
Implication: Raises urgent AI governance and national security concerns

title: ICC Introduces Landmark Maternity Policy Allowing Cricketers to Play Through Motherhood
summary: The International Cricket Council (ICC) has announced a landmark policy affirming that motherhood and professional cricket can coexist, introducing provisions for women cricketers who are pregnant or have recently given birth. The policy is expected to include provisions for maternity leave, return-to-play protocols, and protections against contract termination due to pregnancy. This follows similar moves by other global sports bodies and is seen as a major step for gender equity in cricket. The policy applies to all ICC member boards.
exam_angle: Relevant as a current affairs item on gender equity in sports, international sports governance, and women's rights; connects to GS Paper 1 (Social Issues) and GS Paper 2 (IR/governance of international bodies).
key_facts: Body: International Cricket Council (ICC)
Policy: Maternity policy for women cricketers — pregnancy/maternity leave protections
Significance: Landmark step for gender equity in international cricket
Applies to: All ICC member cricket boards globally

title: CBSE Frames New Class 12 Assessment Policy for Students in Gulf Countries
summary: The Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) has informed the Supreme Court that it has framed a new assessment policy specifically for Class 12 students studying in CBSE-affiliated schools in Gulf countries. The move follows concerns raised about the evaluation framework for Indian students abroad, particularly regarding disruptions caused by geopolitical or administrative factors in the Gulf region. The policy aims to ensure equitable and standardised assessment for the Indian diaspora studying under the CBSE curriculum. CBSE currently has affiliated schools in over 25 countries.
exam_angle: Relevant to GS Paper 2 on education governance, the role of CBSE as a regulatory body, and issues concerning Indian diaspora education; the Supreme Court oversight angle is also important.
key_facts: Body: CBSE (Central Board of Secondary Education)
Action: New assessment policy for Class 12 students in Gulf countries
Forum: Informed to the Supreme Court of India
CBSE: Affiliated schools in 25+ countries globally

title: India's Moneycontrol Eco Pulse Rises to 54.5 in May, Signalling Economic Recovery
summary: India's Moneycontrol Eco Pulse index rose to 54.5 in May 2026, indicating a broad-based economic comeback after a period of moderation. A reading above 50 signals expansion in economic activity. The index tracks multiple high-frequency indicators including manufacturing output, services activity, credit growth, and consumer demand. The improvement suggests India's economy is recovering momentum, though foreign institutional investor (FII) outflows remain a concern amid global uncertainty.
exam_angle: Relevant to GS Paper 3 (Economy) on high-frequency economic indicators, India's growth trajectory, and the divergence between domestic economic performance and foreign investor sentiment.
key_facts: Index: Moneycontrol Eco Pulse
May 2026 reading: 54.5 (above 50 = expansion zone)
Tracks: Manufacturing, services, credit growth, consumer demand
Concern: Foreign investor outflows despite domestic economic recovery
